24.reason: Error: Loop condition should be i < num, not i <= num, because num is the index of the last element read, so the array has num elements (indices 0 to num-1). Using <= causes out-of-bounds access.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be i < num, not i <= num, because num is the index of the last element read, so the array has num elements (indices 0 to num-1). Using <= causes out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be i < num, not i <= num, because num is the index of the last element read, so the array has num+1 elements but the last element (index num) was not properly initialized (the while loop increments num after reading, so the last valid index is num-1). This causes reading uninitialized data.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Error: Loop bound should be i < num. num is the count of terms, so valid indices are 0 to num-1. Using <= causes out-of-bounds access to uninitialized memory and may cause compilation/runtime errors in strict C standards.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  C89 standard does not allow variable declaration inside for-loop initialization, which causes a compilation error on strict or older compilers.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition should be i < num, not i <= num, because num is the count of elements and indices go from 0 to num-1]

31.reason: The loop condition p<=sum causes an out‑of‑bounds access to final[sum] when p reaches sum. This compares temppow with the uninitialized final[sum].pow, potentially leading to incorrect combining or duplication.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be p < sum, not p <= sum, because sum is the current count of elements in final array. Using <= causes out-of-bounds access when p equals sum.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be p < sum, not p <= sum, because sum is the current count of elements in final array. When p == sum, final[p] is uninitialized, causing undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition should be p<sum; when sum=0, accesses uninitialized final[0]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition p<=sum causes an out‑of‑bounds access to final[sum] when p reaches sum. This compares temppow with the uninitialized final[sum].pow, potentially leading to incorrect combining or duplication.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Error: The 'final' array is uninitialized. When sum is 0, final[0].pow contains garbage values, causing incorrect equality checks and logic failures.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  C89 standard does not allow variable declaration inside for-loop initialization, which causes a compilation error on strict or older compilers.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition should be p < sum when checking existing terms; also, when sum==0, this loop doesn't run, so first term is never added correctly]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop condition should be p < sum when checking existing terms; also, when sum==0, this loop doesn't run, so new terms aren't added correctly]
